info.title: Triton Cloud Control Plane
info.description: Triton Cloud control-plane API. Phase 0 surface (`/v2/health`); subsequent phases add silos, instances, VPCs, audit, secrets, and the rest of DESIGN.md §14.

get.summary: Page through audit events. Returns at most `limit` events with
get.description: `seq > after_seq` plus the current chain head.

parameters.name: after_seq
parameters.description: Return events with `seq > after_seq`. Default 0 (start of chain).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum events to return. Default 100, max 1000.

get.summary: Fetch a single audit event by sequence number.
Walk the audit chain in `[from, to]` and recompute hashes.
get.description: Returns the first divergence (if any) plus the current head. Cheap on small ranges; auditors typically walk the entire chain once per export.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Last seq to walk. Default = current chain head.

get.summary: List the calling user's API keys. The plaintext secret is never

post.summary: Create a long-lived API key for the calling user. The plaintext
post.description: secret is included in the response **once** and never shown again.

delete.summary: Delete one of the calling user's API keys. Returns 404 if the
delete.description: id does not belong to the calling user.

post.summary: Exchange username + password for an access/refresh token pair.
Exchange a valid refresh token for a fresh access/refresh pair.
post.description: Returns 401 if the refresh token is invalid or expired.

get.summary: Liveness check. Returns service status and version string.

post.summary: Create a silo. Returns 201 with the created silo.
post.description: Fails with 409 if a silo with the requested name already exists.

get.summary: Look up a silo by id. Returns 404 if no such silo exists.
Read the OIDC IdP config for a silo. The client secret is
get.description: never returned. 404 when no IdP is configured.

post.summary: Configure the OIDC identity provider for a silo. Returns 502
post.description: if the discovery document cannot be fetched, 404 if the silo does not exist, otherwise 201 with the redacted view of what was persisted.

delete.summary: Remove the OIDC IdP config for a silo. Federated users in
delete.description: that silo will fail to authenticate until a new config is posted.

get.summary: List the images registered in a silo's catalog.

post.summary: Register an image in a silo's catalog. Returns 400 if
post.description: `sha256` is not 64 lowercase hex chars or if `size_bytes` is zero. Returns 409 if the name is already in use within the silo.

get.summary: Read a single image. Returns 404 when the image does not
get.description: exist or belongs to a different silo.

delete.summary: Delete an image. Returns 404 when the image does not exist
delete.description: or belongs to a different silo.

get.summary: List the projects inside a silo.

post.summary: Create a project in a silo. Returns 409 if the project name
post.description: is already in use within that silo.

get.summary: Read a single project. Returns 404 when the project does not
get.description: exist or belongs to a different silo (cross-silo probes do not learn that other silos exist).

delete.summary: Delete a project. Returns 404 when the project does not exist
delete.description: or belongs to a different silo.

get.summary: List FloatingIps owned by a project.

post.summary: Allocate a FloatingIp from the requested family's pool.
post.description: Returns 409 if the name is already in use within the project. Returns 404 if the project does not exist or belongs to a different silo. The returned FloatingIp starts unattached.

get.summary: Read a single FloatingIp. Returns 404 if the FloatingIp
get.description: does not exist or belongs to a different silo or project.

delete.summary: Release a FloatingIp back to its pool. Returns 409 if the
delete.description: FloatingIp is currently attached (operator must detach first).

post.summary: Atomically attach a FloatingIp to a NIC, replacing any
post.description: existing attachment. The target NIC must live in the same silo + project as the FloatingIp; mismatch surfaces as 404.

post.summary: Detach a FloatingIp from its current NIC. Idempotent — a
post.description: detach on an already-detached FloatingIp is a no-op that returns the current record.

get.summary: List instances in a project.

post.summary: Create an instance in a project.
post.description:
Returns 400 if `cpu == 0` or `memory_bytes == 0`. Returns 404 if any referenced resource (image, subnet, ssh-keys) does not exist or lives outside this silo/project. Returns 409 if the instance name is already taken in the project.

Phase 0 ships synchronous lifecycle: the create handler transitions the new instance through Pending → Running before responding. A future slice introduces an async provisioning queue + stub executor; the API surface stays the same but tests will observe Pending-then-Running transitions instead of an instant Running.

get.summary: Read a single instance.

delete.summary: Delete an instance. Returns 409 if the instance is not in
delete.description: a deletable state (must be Stopped or Failed). Returns 404 if the instance does not exist or belongs to a different silo or project.

get.summary: List the Disks attached to an instance. Phase 0 produces
get.description: exactly one (the auto-created `"boot"`); future multi-disk attach lands as a follow-on slice.

get.summary: Read a single Disk. Returns 404 if the Disk does not exist
get.description: or belongs to a different silo, project, or instance.

get.summary: List the NICs attached to an instance. Phase 0 produces
get.description: exactly one (the auto-created `"primary"`); a future slice adds NIC attach/detach.

get.summary: Read a single NIC. Returns 404 if the NIC does not exist or
get.description: belongs to a different silo, project, or instance.

post.summary: Restart a running instance. Returns 409 if the instance is
Start a stopped instance. Returns 409 if the instance is
Stop a running instance. Returns 409 if the instance is

get.summary: Read a project's quota. Returns 404 when the project does
get.description: not exist, lives in a different silo, or has no quota set (no record means "unlimited").

put.summary: Set (or replace) the resource quota on a project. Returns
put.description: 404 when the project does not exist or belongs to a different silo. The server assigns `updated_at`. Quotas are not enforced in Phase 0; the record is stored for the eventual instance-create flow to consult.

delete.summary: Remove a project's quota (project becomes unlimited).
delete.description: Returns 404 when the project does not exist, lives in a different silo, or had no quota set.

get.summary: List the VPCs inside a project. Returns 404 when the silo or
get.description: project does not exist (or the project belongs to a different silo).

post.summary: Create a VPC in a project. Returns 400 if neither `ipv4_block`
post.description: nor `ipv6_block` is provided. Returns 409 if a VPC with the same name already exists in the project. The server assigns `id`, `vni` (random in `[4096, 2^24)`, unique rack-wide), and `created_at`.

get.summary: Read a single VPC. Returns 404 when the VPC does not exist or
get.description: belongs to a different silo or project (cross-tenant probes do not learn that the resource exists).

delete.summary: Delete a VPC. Returns 404 when the VPC does not exist or
delete.description: belongs to a different silo or project. Returns 409 if the VPC still has subnets attached — operators must clear subnets before deleting the parent VPC (Phase 0 has no cascade).

get.summary: List the subnets inside a VPC. Returns 404 when the silo,
get.description: project, or VPC does not exist (or is in the wrong parent).

post.summary: Create a subnet in a VPC. Returns 400 if neither
post.description: `ipv4_block` nor `ipv6_block` is provided. Returns 409 if a subnet with the same name already exists in the VPC, if a CIDR is not contained in the parent VPC's matching-family CIDR, or if a CIDR overlaps an existing subnet's CIDR. The server assigns `id` and `created_at`.

get.summary: Read a single subnet. Returns 404 when the subnet does not
get.description: exist or belongs to a different silo, project, or VPC.

delete.summary: Delete a subnet. Returns 404 when the subnet does not exist
delete.description: or belongs to a different silo, project, or VPC.

get.summary: List the SSH keys registered in a silo's catalog.

post.summary: Register an SSH key in a silo's catalog. The server parses
post.description: `public_key` as openssh format and computes the SHA-256 fingerprint. Returns 400 if the key cannot be parsed, 409 if the name or fingerprint is already in use within the silo.

get.summary: Read a single SSH key. Returns 404 when the key does not
get.description: exist or belongs to a different silo.

delete.summary: Delete an SSH key. Returns 404 when the key does not exist
delete.description: or belongs to a different silo.

oneOf.description: Authenticated operator with an `is_root` flag captured at the time of the event.
Authenticated via API key. `key_id` lets auditors trace the credential without revealing it.
No credential presented. Used only for events where logging the unauthenticated principal is intentional (e.g. login attempts; not for routine deny-on-anonymous probes).
Internal system action (bootstrap, cron, replication).

AddressFamily.description: Address family selector used by [`NewFloatingIp`] to ask the server to allocate from one or the other Phase 0 pool.

ApiKeyCreated.description:
Response body for `POST /v2/auth/api-keys`.

`secret` is the wire-form key. It is shown to the operator **once**; the server retains only a bcrypt hash.

ApiKeyView.description: Wire-safe view of an [`ApiKey`]: identifying metadata, no hash.

AttachFloatingIpRequest.description: Body for `POST /attach`. Names the target NIC the FloatingIp should swap onto. The server resolves silo + project + instance from the NIC; cross-tenant targets surface as 404.

AuditEvent.description: Materialised audit event. The `hash` is `SHA-256(canonical-JSON of every field except hash itself)` so a verifier reading any prefix of the chain can recompute and compare.

ChainHead.description: Snapshot of where the chain currently ends.

hash.description: Its `hash` — the value the next append will use as `prev_hash`.

seq.description: Sequence number of the most recently appended event.

Decision.description: Cedar decision recorded with the event.

oneOf.description: Cedar permitted the action.
Cedar denied the action.
Decision was not evaluated (e.g. recorded before Cedar runs).

Disk.description:
Per-instance persistent storage. Phase 0 auto-creates a single boot disk per instance, sized to the source image and tagged with that image's id. The disk record is the metadata view; the actual content (zfs dataset, mantafs object, etc.) is materialized by the agent at provisioning time.

Multi-disk attach (data disks beyond boot) lands as a follow-on slice. The wire shape here is stable across that change — a `kind: Data` disk is a strict superset of what `Boot` carries once `source_image_id` is allowed to be `None`.

size_bytes.description: Total size in bytes. Boot disks default to `image.size_bytes`; future data disks accept an explicit operator-supplied size.

source_image_id.description: Image the boot disk was sourced from. `Some` for `Boot` disks that came from a registered image; `None` for blank `Data` disks (Phase 0 has no callers that produce `None`, but the type allows it for the multi-disk slice).

DiskKind.description: What a disk is for. Phase 0 ships only `Boot` disks (auto-created from the instance's image at instance create time). `Data` is reserved for the future multi-disk attach slice.

oneOf.description: Bootable; sourced from an Image. The instance boots from this disk on first start.
Blank persistent storage attached to the instance. Reserved for the future multi-disk slice; not yet exercised.

FloatingIp.description:
Tenant-managed external IP, allocated from a fleet pool and attachable to any NIC in the same project. Persists independent of any single instance: when the attached instance is deleted, the `attached_to` field clears but the FloatingIp itself stays owned by the project and reusable.

Each FloatingIp represents *one* address — the wire shape does not bifurcate IPv4 and IPv6. The address family is implicit in the `address` bits.

Phase 0 invariants vs other clouds:

* **Project-owned, not instance-owned.** Instance delete → auto-detach, never auto-release. (AWS detaches but leaves the IP at the account level, which loses the project-scoping story; we keep the project envelope.) * **Symmetric IPv4 + IPv6.** No "v6 floating IPs are a separate type" wart. * **Atomic attach replaces.** A second attach with a different NIC swaps the binding in one transaction; no detach-then-attach window in the control plane. * **Delete is explicit.** Detaching does not auto-release; the FloatingIp persists and is visible in `tcadm` listings until the operator deletes it.

address.description: The actual external address. Allocated at create time from the requested family's pool. Immutable for the life of the record.

attached_to.description: Currently-bound NIC, or `None` if floating. Replaced atomically by `attach`; cleared by `detach` and by the instance-delete cascade.

FloatingIpAttachment.description: Where a [`FloatingIp`] is currently attached. `None` (i.e. the `FloatingIp::attached_to` field is `None`) means the IP is allocated to the project but not bound to any NIC — it persists across attach/detach cycles and across instance deletes.

IdpConfigView.description: Wire-safe view of an [`IdpConfig`] with the client secret redacted. Returned by `GET /v2/silos/{silo_id}/idp`.

Image.description:
Tenant image catalog entry. Phase 0 ships only the metadata record — image content lives in mantafs / object storage and is not modelled here. Operators register images by URL + sha256 and trust the caller for the content match; an eventual import pipeline will pre-stage content in storage and verify the digest before the record is persisted.

Silo-scoped: each silo has its own catalog. A future slice may add a fleet-shared catalog (operator-owned) that silos can reference; for now images are tenant-private.

os.description: OS family identifier (e.g. `linux`, `windows`, `smartos`). Stringly-typed in Phase 0; will tighten to an enum once the instance brand model lands.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the image content. Server-validated for length (64 chars) and charset at create time.

source_url.description: Optional URL where the image content can be fetched. `None` means the content is registered out-of-band (e.g. already in mantafs at a known path resolved by image_id).

version.description: OS version / distro tag (e.g. `ubuntu-22.04`, `windows-server-2022`). Free-form for Phase 0.

Instance.description:
Tenant compute instance. Project-scoped; references one image (boot media), one subnet (network attach point), and zero-or-more SSH keys (injected into authorized_keys at provisioning time).

Phase 0 ships only the metadata + lifecycle state machine. The actual provisioning is faked synchronously inside the create handler; a future slice introduces the intent queue and stub executor that will become the swap-out point for a real `tritonagent`.

Several fields that real cloud instances carry are deliberately omitted in v0: cloud-init userdata, tags/labels, brand (zone/hvm/lx/bhyve), affinity rules, console URL, migration history. Each will land as the consuming use case ships.

image_id.description: Boot image; must be in the same silo as the instance.

primary_subnet_id.description: Subnet the instance's primary NIC attaches to. Phase 0 auto-creates a NIC at provisioning time; a future slice adds explicit NIC records that operators can manage separately. Subnet must live in a VPC inside this project.

ssh_key_ids.description: SSH keys to inject into the instance's authorized_keys at first boot. Each key id must live in the same silo as the instance. May be empty (no key injection).

LifecycleState.description:
Lifecycle state of a tenant instance. The state machine:

```text create ↓ Pending ──→ Provisioning ──┬→ Running ←──┬── Stopped │             │      ↑ │             │      │ ↓             ↓      │ Failed        Stopping─┘ ```

Phase 0 collapses Pending → Provisioning → Running into a synchronous transition inside the create handler (there is no agent yet). The full async path lands when the provisioning intent queue + stub executor slice ships.

Operator-driven transitions: start (Stopped → Pending), stop (Running → Stopping), restart (Running → Stopping → Pending). Agent-driven transitions: Pending → Provisioning → Running, Provisioning → Failed, Stopping → Stopped.

Delete is allowed only from Stopped or Failed; deleting a Running instance returns 409.

oneOf.description: Newly created; not yet picked up by an agent.
An agent has claimed the provisioning job and is working.
Up and running.
Stop requested; agent is winding down.
Fully stopped; safe to delete or restart.
Unrecoverable error. Inspect `reason`; the instance must be deleted (no in-place recovery in Phase 0).

LoginRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/auth/login`.

`password` is a [`RedactedString`] so a stray `Debug` of this struct does not print the credential and so the in-memory copy is zeroed when the value drops.

NewFloatingIp.description: Request body for allocating a new FloatingIp. The server picks the actual address from the family-specific Phase 0 pool; the caller asks for `V4` or `V6` and gets the lowest free address.

NewIdpConfig.description: Request body for `POST /v2/silos/{silo_id}/idp`. tritond **eagerly** fetches the IdP's discovery document on this call; a 4xx/5xx return means the IdP isn't reachable or doesn't speak OIDC, and the config is not persisted.

audience.description: Expected `aud` claim. Defaults to `client_id` when omitted.

NewImage.description: Request body for registering an image in a silo's catalog. The owning silo comes from the URL path. The server assigns `id` and `created_at`.

NewInstance.description: Request body for creating an instance. The owning silo + project come from the URL path. The server assigns `id`, initial `lifecycle`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`.

NewProject.description: Request body for creating a project. The owning silo comes from the URL path, not the body.

NewQuota.description: Request body for setting a project's quota. The owning silo and project come from the URL path. The server assigns `updated_at`.

NewSilo.description:
Request body for creating a silo.

Distinct from [`Silo`] because the server assigns `id` and `created_at`. `description` is optional on the wire and stored as an empty string when omitted.

NewSshKey.description: Request body for registering a new SSH key in a silo's catalog. The server assigns `id`, `fingerprint`, and `created_at`; the owning silo comes from the URL path.

NewSubnet.description: Request body for creating a subnet. The owning silo, project, and VPC come from the URL path. The server assigns `id` and `created_at`. At least one of `ipv4_block` / `ipv6_block` must be `Some`; the API rejects requests with both `None`.

NewVpc.description: Request body for creating a VPC. The owning silo + project come from the URL path, not the body. The server assigns `id`, `vni`, and `created_at`. At least one of `ipv4_block` / `ipv6_block` must be `Some`; the API rejects requests with both `None`.

Nic.description:
Per-instance network interface. Auto-created at instance create time with a single "primary" NIC; multi-NIC attach/detach is a future slice. Mirrors what the dataplane (OPTE per-NIC config) will eventually consume — guest MAC, primary IPv4/IPv6 in the subnet's CIDR.

Phase 0 stores only the per-NIC fields the agent needs to open the overlay attachment for an instance:

* `mac` — guest MAC address. Locally-administered (`02:` prefix) + 5 random bytes; uniqueness is rack-wide. * `primary_ipv4` / `primary_ipv6` — addresses allocated from the parent subnet's CIDR. Each family is `Some` only if the subnet has a CIDR for that family.

External IPs, attached_subnets (other-subnet routing), and firewall rules are *not* on the NIC record — they live on future External-IP / Route / FirewallRule resources.

mac.description: Guest MAC, formatted as 6 lowercase hex bytes separated by colons (e.g. `02:1a:b3:cd:ef:42`).

primary_ipv4.description: Primary IPv4 from `subnet.ipv4_block`. `None` if the parent subnet is IPv6-only.

primary_ipv6.description: Primary IPv6 from `subnet.ipv6_block`. `None` if the parent subnet is IPv4-only.

Outcome.description: Outcome of the action after the handler finished.

oneOf.description: Handler completed successfully. `resource` carries the affected entity uid when known (e.g. `Silo::"<uuid>"`).
Cedar denied; handler did not run.
Authentication failed (bad password, expired token, missing key).
Caller-side error (4xx other than 401/403).
Server-side error (5xx).

Project.description: Sub-tenancy boundary inside a silo. Workload resources (instances, volumes, networks) eventually nest under projects. Phase 0e-c carries only the bare-minimum identifying fields; quota envelopes and per-project Cedar bindings come in later slices.

Quota.description:
Per-project resource quota. Singleton: each project has at most one quota record. Set with PUT, read with GET, remove with DELETE (project becomes "unlimited" — no record means no enforcement).

Enforcement is *not* shipped in Phase 0 — these limits are stored and readable but no instance-create flow consults them yet. The shape is fixed now so the enforcement layer (Tier 3) has a stable contract to build against.

Limits are absolute caps, not reservations: `cpu_limit = 8` means "this project may have up to 8 vCPUs across all running instances." Storage and memory are bytes; cpu and instance counts are simple `u32`.

cpu_limit.description: Maximum total vCPUs across all instances in the project.

disk_bytes.description: Maximum total disk across all instances + volumes in the project, in bytes.

instance_limit.description: Maximum number of running instances in the project.

memory_bytes.description: Maximum total memory across all instances in the project, in bytes.

Silo.description: A tenancy boundary. Every Triton Cloud resource ultimately rolls up to a silo; in production each silo is bound to its own identity provider. Phase 0 carries only the bare-minimum identifying fields.

SshKey.description:
User-supplied SSH public key, registered in a silo's catalog so instance launches can pick keys to inject into authorized_keys. Phase 0 is silo-scoped (any user in the silo can pick from the pool). A future slice may add per-user ownership; the silo_id field is forward-compatible with that.

The server validates the openssh wire format at create time and computes the SHA-256 fingerprint. The raw `public_key` string is stored verbatim so it can be handed to cloud-init without reformatting.

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint, e.g. `SHA256:abcd...`. Server-computed at create time and stored alongside the key for cheap display / lookup. Never accepted on the wire.

public_key.description: OpenSSH-formatted public key — `<algo> <base64> [comment]`. Server-validated at create time; rejected with 400 if the `ssh-key` crate refuses to parse it.

Subnet.description:
Layer-3 subnet inside a VPC. Each subnet carves a CIDR out of its parent VPC's IPv4 and/or IPv6 block. Multiple subnets may exist per VPC; their CIDRs must not overlap. NIC attach points to a specific subnet at instance-launch time.

Invariants enforced at create time: * Every present subnet CIDR must be a strict subnet of the parent VPC's same-family CIDR (`ipv4_block ⊆ vpc.ipv4_block`, `ipv6_block ⊆ vpc.ipv6_block`). * No subnet CIDR (in either family) may overlap an existing subnet CIDR in the same VPC. * At least one of `ipv4_block` / `ipv6_block` must be `Some`, and each present family must also be present on the parent VPC (an IPv4-only VPC cannot host an IPv6 subnet).

ipv4_block.description: IPv4 CIDR for this subnet. Must be a subnet of the parent VPC's `ipv4_block`, and must not overlap any other subnet's IPv4 CIDR in the same VPC.

ipv6_block.description: IPv6 CIDR for this subnet. Must be a subnet of the parent VPC's `ipv6_block`, and must not overlap any other subnet's IPv6 CIDR in the same VPC.

TokenResponse.description: Response body for both login and refresh.

oneOf.description: The walked range is internally consistent and chains back to `from - 1` correctly. `verified_to` is the highest seq reached.

Found a hash mismatch. `seq` is the first event whose stored hash does not match its recomputed value, or whose `prev_hash` does not match the prior event's `hash`. The chain bytes themselves remain queryable; the operator decides what to do.

Vpc.description: Tenant VPC. Project-scoped (Phase 1 URL shape). Mirrors the per-VPC fields OPTE consumes in `oxide_vpc::api::VpcCfg`: a 24-bit Geneve VNI plus an optional primary IPv4 CIDR and optional primary IPv6 CIDR (matching OPTE's `IpCfg` enum: Ipv4-only, Ipv6-only, or dual-stack). Per-NIC concerns (guest MAC, private IPs, external IPs, attached_subnets, DHCP) are *not* on the VPC record — those land on subnet/instance/NIC resources in later slices.

ipv4_block.description: Primary IPv4 CIDR for the VPC overlay. `None` for IPv6-only. Wire format is the canonical CIDR string, e.g. `"10.0.0.0/24"`.

ipv6_block.description: Primary IPv6 CIDR for the VPC overlay. `None` for IPv4-only. Wire format is the canonical CIDR string, e.g. `"fd00::/48"`.

vni.description: Geneve Virtual Network Identifier. Server-assigned at create time, drawn from `[VPC_VNI_RESERVED_CEILING, VPC_VNI_MAX)`, unique rack-wide.
